data.title: Amundi Capitalizes on Equity Market Selloff Amid Iran Conflict Concerns
data.description: Amundi, a leading asset management firm, has strategically increased its investments in equity markets during a period of widespread selloff. Bloomberg posted on X that this move comes as many investors have been reducing their stock holdings due to heightened fears of an escalation in the conflict involving Iran. Amundi's decision to buy into the market downturn has proven to be beneficial, as the firm capitalized on the situation while others were retreating. This approach highlights Amundi's confidence in the market's resilience despite geopolitical tensions.

data.title: Micron Technology Leads Storage Sector Gains with Over 10% Surge
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) experienced a significant increase in its stock price, leading the storage sector with a rise of over 10% in pre-market trading on April 8. According to BlockBeats, the stock surged to 11.53% during the trading session. SanDisk (SNDK) also saw a concurrent rise, increasing by 9%. On the Hyperliquid platform, MU was reported at $417.3, while SNDK was at $775.1.

The largest bullish address on the platform, identified as 0xa65, initiated positions in MU and SNDK on March 31, with average entry prices of $328.3 and $665.4, respectively. The total holding size is approximately $9.55 million, with current unrealized gains reaching $1.9 million.

data.title: U.S. and Iran Reach Fragile Ceasefire Agreement
data.description:
On April 8, U.S. Vice President Vance attended an event in Hungary, where he described the ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran as a 'fragile truce.' According to BlockBeats, Vance did not provide further details but mentioned that the agreement includes a ceasefire, the advancement of negotiations, and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

Vance stated that U.S. President Donald Trump has instructed the American representatives involved in negotiations with Iran to proceed 'in good faith,' while also expressing Trump's eagerness for progress. He further noted that the United States is prepared to use 'extraordinary economic leverage,' although Trump has directed that these measures not be employed at this time.

Vance emphasized that if Iran engages in negotiations 'in good faith,' there is potential for an agreement, but ultimately, the outcome of the talks depends on the Iranians.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guard to Announce Strategic Statement for Operation
data.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps is set to release a strategic statement for the 100th round of the 'Real Commitment-4' operation. According to Odaily, this announcement is expected soon. Further details about the operation have not been disclosed.

data.title: China's Financial Regulatory Authority Issues Guidelines for Rural Financial Support
data.description: On April 8, Jin10 reported that China's National Financial Regulatory Administration released a notice titled 'Guidelines for Enhancing Financial Support for Comprehensive Rural Revitalization in 2026.' According to Jin10, the notice emphasizes the need to optimize the rural financial service environment, promote the construction of rural credit systems, and enhance the integration of rural finance with village governance. It also highlights the importance of strengthening the monitoring of agricultural credit risks and preventing the misuse of agricultural loans for non-agricultural purposes. The notice strictly prohibits the illegal increase of local government hidden debts under the guise of agricultural finance and warns against collusion with illegal intermediaries to fraudulently obtain loans and insurance claims. Banks are urged to enhance the daily supervision and management of rural inclusive financial service points to effectively prevent risks associated with illegal fundraising and other unlawful financial activities.

data.title: SBI Ripple Asia Completes XRP Ledger Token Issuance System for Prepaid Payments
data.description: SBI Ripple Asia has finalized the development of a token issuance system on the XRP Ledger, specifically for external prepaid payment instruments. According to NS3.AI, the system is designed to align with the existing Payment Services Act. SBI Ripple Asia successfully completed its regulatory registration as a third-party prepaid payment instrument issuer on March 26, 2026.

data.title: Hyperliquid Contributor Opens Leveraged Short Position on WTIOIL
data.description:
On April 8, a significant trading move was detected involving Hyperliquid's early contributor, Loracle. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the address (0x8def…) established a leveraged short position on WTIOIL (WTI crude oil) with a 10x leverage. The position involved 190,738.62 contracts, with an average opening price of $93.68.

The current liquidation price for this position is set at $100.53, while the current CL price stands at $93.34. This short position is currently experiencing a slight unrealized profit of $20,000.

data.title: US Vice President Vance: The US-Iran ceasefire is a "fragile truce," and Trump is "eager to see progress."
data.description: On April 8 local time, US Vice President Vance, speaking at an event in Hungary, stated that the ceasefire reached between the US and Iran was a "fragile truce." He did not reveal further details, only stating that the agreement included a ceasefire, advancing negotiations, and reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Vance said that US President Trump had asked the US representative responsible for negotiations with Iran to conduct consultations "in good faith," but also said that Trump was "eager to see progress." He further stated that the US was prepared to use "extraordinary economic leverage," but Trump had instructed the US to "not use these means for the time being." Vance said that if Iran negotiates "in good faith," the US might be able to reach an agreement, but "how the negotiations ultimately proceed depends on the Iranians." (CCTV News)

data.title: Iran's Navy Enforces Strait of Hormuz Transit Restrictions Amid Tensions
data.description: Iran's navy has announced that vessels near the Strait of Hormuz must obtain permission to transit the waterway. According to NS3.AI, this directive comes as U.S. President Donald Trump linked a two-week suspension of strikes to the immediate, full, and safe reopening of the strait. An audio recording shared by a crew member warned that any vessel attempting to pass without permission would be destroyed. Photos and videos from crew members depicted fighter jets over the Persian Gulf, with most vessels remaining anchored.

data.description: The whale initiated a 3x BTC short position with a value of $1.755 million, setting a target of 10 large targets.

data.title: Bitcoin Whale Executes Triple Short Position
data.description:
On April 8, a significant Bitcoin trader known as 'Set 10 Big Goals First' (@Jason60704294) revealed a triple short position on Bitcoin. According to BlockBeats, the current short position amounts to 24.488 BTC.

Earlier this morning, the trader closed a previous long position.

data.title: First Ships Pass Through Strait of Hormuz After U.S.-Iran Ceasefire
data.description:
On April 8, ship tracking website MarineTraffic recorded the first vessel movements through the Strait of Hormuz following the U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement. According to BlockBeats, the Greek-flagged bulk carrier 'NJ Earth' passed through the strait at 8:44 GMT, while the Liberian-flagged 'Daytona Beach' crossed at 6:59 GMT, having departed from the Port of Abbas at 5:28 GMT.

MarineTraffic data indicates that the region still hosts hundreds of vessels, including 426 oil tankers, 34 liquefied petroleum gas carriers, and 19 liquefied natural gas carriers.

data.title: The Washington Post: The Trump administration is expected to drastically cut its funding request for the war with Iran.
data.description: According to the Washington Post, the Trump administration is expected to drastically reduce its funding requests for the war with Iran. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Government Plans Significant Reduction in Iran War Funding Request
data.description: The Trump administration is set to significantly reduce its funding request for the Iran war, according to BlockBeats. The Washington Post reported on April 8 that the U.S. government is planning to cut back on its financial demands related to military operations in Iran. This decision marks a notable shift in the administration's approach to the conflict, reflecting a change in priorities or strategy. The implications of this reduction in funding are yet to be fully understood, but it could signal a move towards de-escalation or a reallocation of resources to other areas.

data.title: SEC Chair Ends Enforcement-Based Regulation Approach
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chair, Paul Atkins, has announced the termination of the enforcement-based regulatory approach. According to Foresight News, Atkins criticized the previous administration's handling of seven crypto registration cases, describing them as a 'misallocation of resources' that did not directly benefit investors.

data.title: South Korea's Democratic Party Proposes Legal Framework for Tokenized Assets and Stablecoins
data.description:
South Korea's Democratic Party is moving to integrate tokenized real-world assets (RWA) and stablecoins into the existing legal framework. According to ChainCatcher, the proposal includes provisions in the draft of the Digital Asset Basic Act.

The proposal mandates that issuers of tokenized RWAs deposit linked assets into trust accounts in accordance with the Capital Markets Act. Stablecoins are classified as 'means of payment' under the Foreign Exchange Transactions Act, to be regulated by foreign exchange authorities without requiring additional registration. Small stablecoin transactions are exempt from foreign exchange reporting, while larger transactions remain subject to regulatory requirements.

Additionally, the proposal prohibits offering returns on idle stablecoin balances and requires the Financial Services Commission to establish technical standards for stablecoin interoperability. The Digital Asset Basic Act represents South Korea's second set of digital asset regulations, which has faced multiple legislative delays, pushing the original 2025 launch timeline further back.

data.title: Two whales bought over 250,000 HYPE tokens together.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, whales are buying HYPE. One whale deposited 7.86 million USDC into Hyperliquid and bought 200,042 HYPE at a price of $39.3. Additionally, Cooker.hl (@CookerFlips) bought 50,751 HYPE at a price of $38.5, worth $1.99 million.

data.title: Iran to Participate in Islamabad Talks, Confirms President
data.description: On April 8, Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced that Iranian President Ebrahim Raisi has confirmed Iran's participation in the upcoming Islamabad talks. According to BlockBeats, this development marks a significant step in diplomatic relations between the two countries. The talks are expected to address various bilateral and regional issues, fostering cooperation and dialogue. The confirmation from Iran underscores the importance of these discussions in strengthening ties and addressing mutual concerns.

data.title: U.S. Vice President Vance Announces Iran's Agreement on Strait of Hormuz
data.description: U.S. Vice President Vance has announced that Iran has agreed to open the Strait of Hormuz. According to Odaily, this development is significant for international trade and maritime navigation, as the strait is a crucial passage for global oil shipments. The agreement is expected to ease tensions in the region and facilitate smoother transit for vessels. Further details on the terms of the agreement have not been disclosed.

data.title: Middle East Ceasefire May Boost Risk Appetite, Expert Cautions Against Premature Optimism
data.description: Stephen Dover, Chief Market Strategist at Franklin Templeton Institute, expressed in a report on April 8 that the Middle East ceasefire is expected to revive risk appetite, though it is too early to declare victory. According to Jin10, Dover emphasized that the appropriate analytical framework should view this as an initial rebound, potentially followed by a reassessment. He advised investors to focus on real-time indicators, noting that crude oil prices, tanker volumes, and shipping conditions will be more revealing than political statements. Dover stated that the ceasefire is evidently beneficial for the market as it directly reduces the risk of inflation and growth shocks triggered by oil. However, given its temporary and conditional nature, this should be interpreted as a relief rebound rather than a clear signal of normalization.
